channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Why OpenAI bought 'SportsCenter for Silicon Valley'
item.description: OpenAI is seeking to shape the public narrative about AI with the purchase of a niche talk show popular with Silicon Valley insiders.
item.content:encoded: OpenAI is seeking to shape the public narrative about AI with the purchase of a niche talk show popular with Silicon Valley insiders.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond

item.title: Oil prices plunge and stocks soar after U.S. and Iran agree on a ceasefire
item.description: Investors around the world breathed a sigh of relief at the prospect of peace — and an easing of the global energy crisis.
item.content:encoded:
Investors around the world breathed a sigh of relief at the prospect of peace — and an easing of the global energy crisis.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan

item.title: A year after 'Liberation Day,' Trump's tariffs are hurting small businesses
item.description: Many small-business owners say the uncertainty that goes along with the high cost of the Trump administration's emergency tariffs is driving away customers and eating into profits.
item.content:encoded:
Many small-business owners say the uncertainty that goes along with the high cost of the Trump administration's emergency tariffs is driving away customers and eating into profits.

(Image credit: Andrew Schneider)
item.dc:creator: Andrew Schneider

item.title: Drive slower, go electric, don't drive at all? Americans weigh options for saving gas
item.description: With gasoline prices averaging above $4 a gallon nationally, drivers are grappling with a sharp rise in fuel costs. Here are some ideas to consider if you're trying to cut your fuel costs.
item.content:encoded:
With gasoline prices averaging above $4 a gallon nationally, drivers are grappling with a sharp rise in fuel costs. Here are some ideas to consider if you're trying to cut your fuel costs.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: How Bill Phillips used flowing water to model the economy
item.description: Bill Phillips was an outsider to economics, but he used a machine and a chart to change the way we think about the government's role in a capitalist economy.
item.content:encoded:
Bill Phillips was an outsider to economics, but he used a machine and a chart to change the way we think about the government's role in a capitalist economy.

(Image credit: Julian Frost for Planet Money: A Guide To The Economic Forces That Shape Your Life
)
item.dc:creator: Alex Mayyasi

item.title: Beer cans, helium balloons and mortgages: An unexpected mix of things affected by war
item.description: It's not just oil and gas that are affected by the Iran war. All sorts of shortages and price spikes are starting to pop up that stand to affect people's daily lives.
item.content:encoded:
It's not just oil and gas that are affected by the Iran war. All sorts of shortages and price spikes are starting to pop up that stand to affect people's daily lives.

(Image credit: Brent Jones)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Gas prices are high. What can you do about it?
item.description: With gasoline prices averaging above $4 a gallon nationally, drivers are grappling with a sharp rise in fuel costs. How can you get the most out of every fill-up?
item.content:encoded: With gasoline prices averaging above $4 a gallon nationally, drivers are grappling with a sharp rise in fuel costs. How can you get the most out of every fill-up?
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Questions to help you get 'financially naked' with your partner
item.description: Having "brutally honest conversations" about money can bring couples closer together, says Vivian Tu, a financial educator. She shares questions to ask your partner at every relationship stage.
item.content:encoded: Having "brutally honest conversations" about money can bring couples closer together, says Vivian Tu, a financial educator. She shares questions to ask your partner at every relationship stage.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: When legal sports betting surges, so do Americans' financial problems
item.description: As online betting has grown in popularity, a new report from the New York Federal Reserve builds on the troubling link between legal sports wagering and financial health.
item.content:encoded:
As online betting has grown in popularity, a new report from the New York Federal Reserve builds on the troubling link between legal sports wagering and financial health.

(Image credit: Charlie Riedel)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: Tax refunds are trending a bit higher this year. Here's how people are spending them
item.description: Some people are splurging. Others are finding that their refunds are being swallowed up by the rising cost of gas.
item.content:encoded:
Some people are splurging. Others are finding that their refunds are being swallowed up by the rising cost of gas.

(Image credit: Frederic J. Brown)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha
